Machine Learning · NCRB India · 2007–2021
S
Sujith SA
ML & Data Science Project

Crime Hotspot
Prediction
using ML

A multi-class classification system trained on 15 years of NCRB data to predict dominant crime categories across Indian states. Six ML models compared — Random Forest achieves the best accuracy at 78.9%.

📊 Year-wise Crime Trend — All categories · 2007 to 2021
Data Visualizations
Project Screenshots & Charts

All charts generated from actual NCRB data covering 36 states and union territories across India.

📈 Year-wise Crime Trend (2007–2021)

All crime categories tracked across 15 years. Cruelty by Husband/Relatives consistently dominates. Sharp spike visible in 2014.

📊 State-wise Total Crimes Against Women

Uttar Pradesh (10.79%) and West Bengal (10.17%) lead all states in reported crimes.

🗺️ Victim Age Group Heatmap by State

The 18–30 age group is most impacted across nearly every state.

🔗 Population vs Crime Correlation

Strong positive correlation (r = 0.81) between population and total crimes.

🏆 Top 5 States — Year-on-Year Comparison

Crime grew significantly 2013→2019→2020. West Bengal and UP show steepest increase.

🧮 Crime Category Correlation Heatmap

Rape and Assault share 0.88 correlation. Cruelty by Relatives: 0.92 with total crimes.

📅 Year-wise Rape Case Trend

Clear spike in 2014 (post-Nirbhaya increased reporting). Gradual upward trend from 2015 to 2021.

Model Evaluation
6 ML Models Compared
✓ Best Model
Random Forest (RFC)
78.9%
Ensemble method, handles noise well. Selected as final model.
XGBoost
76.4%
Strong gradient boosting, slightly slower training.
SVM
74.2%
Strong on linear decision boundaries.
KNN
71.5%
Sensitive to feature scaling and dimensionality.
Decision Tree
68.3%
Interpretable but prone to overfitting.
Logistic Regression
66.1%
Baseline comparison model.
Target Classes
6 Crime Categories Predicted
1
Rape
2
Kidnapping & Abduction
3
Dowry Deaths
4
Assault on Women
5
Cruelty by Husband / Relatives
6
Other IPC Crimes
Analysis Results
Key Findings from the Data
📍

Top Hotspot States

Uttar Pradesh (10.79%) and West Bengal (10.17%) lead all states. Rajasthan ranks third at 8.97%.

📈

Most Prevalent Crime

Cruelty by Husband/Relatives is the highest volume crime category consistently from 2007 to 2021.

👤

Most At-Risk Age Group

The 18–30 age group is the most impacted across nearly every state and crime category.

📅

2014 Spike

A sharp spike in 2014 visible across categories — linked to increased reporting awareness post-Nirbhaya.

🔗

Population Correlation

Strong positive correlation of r = 0.81 between population size and total crime count.

⚡

Crime Co-occurrence

Rape and Assault share 0.88 correlation. Cruelty by Relatives has the strongest link at 0.92.
